title: M-3PO military protocol droid

The '''M-3PO military protocol droid''', also known as the '''standard military protocol unit''', was a variant of [[Cybot Galactica/Legends|Cybot Galactica's]] successful [[3PO-series protocol droid/Legends|3PO-series protocol droid]], designed for military applications. These [[Droid/Legends|droids]] were common sights in [[Planet/Legends|planetary]] garrisons and [[Starfighter/Legends|starfighter]] bases.

==Description==
The M-3PO military protocol [[Droid/Legends|droid]] was produced by [[Cybot Galactica/Legends|Cybot Galactica]] and was a variant of their prolific [[3PO-series protocol droid/Legends|3PO-series protocol droid]] design. The units used standard 3PO-series bodies painted<ref name="EGTD" /> a nonmetallic<ref name="AEG" /> [[Color/Legends|black]], and incorporated [[Clam/Legends|clamshell]]-shaped heads taken from Cybot Galactica's discontinued [[KW traffic controller]]. M-3POs had red [[Photoreceptor/Legends|photoreceptors]] that were nestled in between the upper and lower halves of the head.<ref name="EGTD" />

M-3PO's utilized the same [[AA-1 VerboBrain/Legends|AA-1 VerboBrain]] as standard 3PO units.<ref name="Gamer3" /> All of the droid's communications and [[Sensor/Legends|sensor]] equipment, including [[Audio sensor/Legends|auditory sensors]] were located in the clamshell head. A reset button and [[Olfactory sensor/Legends|olfactory sensors]] were positioned on the neck. Due to the re-use of the standard 3PO chassis, M-3PO droids suffered the same physical limitations including stiff joints and poor balance.<ref name="EGTD" /> The unit's body and head were [[Armor/Legends|armored]]<ref name="Gamer3" /> with [[Plasteel/Legends|plasteel]],<ref name="TotG">''[[Threats of the Galaxy]]''</ref> and M-3POs incorporated a [[Translator/Legends|translator unit]], a [[Holorecorder/Legends|holorecorder]], and a [[Vocabulator/Legends|vocabulator]].<ref name="Gamer3" />

[[File:M3PO_egtd.png|thumb|right|200px|M-3PO schematic]]
Despite being classed as military droids, M-3PO units were not designed to serve in a combat role<ref name="EGTD" /> although they were occasionally used in battle.<ref name="CSWE">{{CSWECite|II|271|M-3PO}}</ref> They were intended to fulfil the role of an administrative organizer, being equipped with a [[Military Database Computer]] that was programmed with the rules and regulations of over six million military and paramilitary organizations, including honor codes, ceremonial protocols, and historical doctrines from militaries past and present.<ref name="EGTD" />

M-3POs were tasked with requisitioning equipment, barracks billeting, duty roster planning and personnel file management. The droids were notoriously thorough, and insisted on maintaining procedure and filing paperwork through the "proper channels". Combined with the usual sluggish bureaucracies that most militaries operated under, it could take an M-3PO unit [[Standard week/Legends|weeks]] to fill a [[Regiment/Legends|regiment's]] simple order for two dozen [[Blaster rifle/Legends|blaster rifles]].<ref name="EGTD" />

==History==
[[File:M-3PO_EGD.jpg|thumb|left|200px|A [[Cybot Galactica/Legends|Cybot Galactica]] M-3PO military [[Protocol droid/Legends|protocol droid]].]]
Following the success of the 3PO-series, Cybot Galactic hoped to capitalise on its accomplishment by producing and selling variants based on the droid. Most never moved past the design stage,<ref name="TotG" /> but a junior executive believed that the military was an untapped market for the 3PO-series and its AA-1 VerboBrain.<ref name="Gamer3" /> Following the introduction of the M-3PO<ref name="EGTD" /> [[Standard century/Legends|centuries]] prior to the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]],<ref name="AEG" /> they soon became common sights in [[Planet/Legends|planetary]] [[Garrison/Legends|garrisons]] and [[Starfighter/Legends|starfighter]] bases across [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]].<ref name="EGTD" /> They did the job they were designed to do ably,<ref name="Gamer3" /> but the [[Soldier/Legends|soldiers]] assigned to the same facilities as an M-3PO droid were often dismayed by their presence.<ref name="EGTD" />

Although the M-3PO units differed in appearance from their 3PO cousins, the soldiers often complained that they were no less annoying.<ref name="EGTD" /> M-3POs retained the same chattery, annoying traits that were the hallmark of the standard 3PO line. The production run was small, and the line was eventually scrapped.<ref name="AEG" /> On more than one occasion, M-3PO droids were abandoned in the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories]] by the soldiers they were assigned to. These rogue units often became soldiers themselves<ref name="Gamer3" /> after taking the initiative to reprogram themselves.<ref name="TotG" />

M-3PO units were commonly found in military installations, including [[Starship/Legends|starships]] and [[Space station/Legends|space stations]]. In addition to their duties requisitioning supplies, organizing duty rosters, and managing personnel, some M-3PO units were used by commanders as strategic aides and training instructors due to their vast military knowledge. These droids gave briefings and lectures, and while the soldiers on the receiving end disliked being trained by M-3POs, the troops who listened and heeded their advice usually returned from combat alive.<ref name="TotG" /> 

During the early [[Standard year/Legends|years]] of the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]], [[Rogue Squadron/Legends|Rogue Squadron]] had an M-3PO unit named [[M-3PO (Emtrey)|Emtrey]] who was the unit's [[Quartermaster/Legends|quartermaster]]. Emtry had been assembled from three junked M-3PO units at [[Echo Base/Legends|Echo Base]] on the planet [[Hoth/Legends|Hoth]], and had been modified to include commodities-brokering software and a secondary personality that acted as a "scrounger", allowing the droid to engage in bribery, bartering, and [[Black market/Legends|black market]] trading to obtain supplies.<ref name="EGTD" />
